title: Clockwork Beetle — Tool Registry
concept: Precise, deterministic tool execution with capability gating
metaphor: Beetles are precision machines — exoskeleton as capability boundary, mandibles as tool grips
platform: Custom tools docs, tool registry explanation
prompt: Highly detailed 18th century copperplate engraving of a large stag beetle viewed from above, its body opened like a technical diagram to reveal an intricate clockwork mechanism inside. Each gear and lever is labeled with tiny text. The beetle's mandibles grip different small tools: a file, a key, a measuring instrument. Around the beetle, a circular arrangement of tool outlines suggests a registry or catalog of available instruments. Hooke's Micrographia style, extreme detail in cross-hatching, stippled textures, black ink on cream paper. Scientific precision. No color.
